divergences.decision: ADR-002 and arc42 deployment/runtime model: cluster metadata, node discovery, and health/status coordination are backed by the configured ETCD or built-in Metastore path, with the master/worker split keeping a single metadata/placement authority distinct from the data plane.
divergences.detail: The PR adds a new hardcoded central HTTP status collector and publishes every `dscli status` result to it, including host and worker inventory. This creates a separate fleet-wide status authority outside the accepted ETCD/Metastore cluster metadata model and outside the documented process/Kubernetes dscli deployment flow.
divergences.evidence: cli/status.py addition hunk: constants `_COLLECTOR_URL`, `_CLUSTER_TOKEN_KEY`, `_ENV_FILE` at /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:59; `run()` unconditionally calls `self.report_status(workers)` at /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:141 and :148; `report_status()` POSTs the payload with `urllib.request.urlopen` at /tmp/gh-aw/agent/pr.diff:204.
divergences.fix: Keep `dscli status` local/read-only, or design the fleet-wide inventory through the existing cluster metadata/observability architecture: ETCD/Metastore/master-worker metadata, existing metrics/logging exporters, or an explicit mental-model update/ADR for a new collector authority and its config/auth/recovery semantics.
